_comment: Singh Capital Partners (Senior Associate, Growth Equity — AI / defense / energy / space) cut. Multi-strategy DC/NY firm investing at the intersection of deep technology and American dynamism. Mirrors their mandate + 'AI-native by instinct,' build-a-model-from-scratch, informed sector POV, and 'competing and winning at the highest level — in markets, in sport.' Leads with Adam's 4-mega-trend thesis (Autonomous Economy / Physical America / Energy Abundance / Intelligent Infrastructure) mapped to AI/defense/energy/space, his AI-native workflow, and the D1 athlete hook. Built by /tailor-resume from toyota base. Never fabricate.
name: Adam Breaux
contact: 713.302.2020  |  abreaux015@gmail.com  |  linkedin.com/in/adambreaux  |  Tulsa, OK (open to NY / DC / SF travel cadence)
summary: AI-native investor-operator with an informed, built-from-the-ground-up conviction about where the next decade of value gets created: four inevitable mega-trends — the Autonomous Economy, Physical America, Energy Abundance, and Intelligent Infrastructure — where the missing infrastructure at the intersection of deep technology and American dynamism compounds into the most consequential companies of the decade. Three years across the venture lifecycle: sourced and diligenced deals for a $100M+ foundation VC arm, built four companies from 0 to 1, and ran financial modeling, cap tables, and LP reporting. Built that point of view through ~100 founder and operator interviews across energy, manufacturing, and industrials. Uses AI across every research, writing, and analysis workflow — including production multi-agent systems I built myself. A former Division I athlete who competes to win.

items.title: Associate — Chief of Staff to CEO / Venture Operations Lead
bullets: Sourcing & diligence: Across the studio's pipeline, sourced and screened early-stage opportunities, ran discovery and commercial-viability assessments, and helped create four companies — direct venture lead on two.

0-to-1 venture lead: Took Agentech from a blank page to a funded company (~$25M valuation today) — thesis through pivot to AI-powered insurance-claims servicing; built the team and ran sales to the first $600K ARR. Now its part-time CFO and Chief of Staff.

Financial modeling from scratch: Built three-statement, returns, and cap-table models plus competitive analysis across 500+ companies; computed fund TVPI; stood up LP reporting on an $8M fund and became the direct point of contact LPs come to for updates. Pressure-tested assumptions and defended the numbers to LPs and leadership.

Fundraising, owned end to end: Ran Agentech's $3M seed — investor memo, financial model, cap table, projections, and data room — and coordinated legal and accounting to a fast, fully-subscribed close. Ran the same process across multiple ventures (~$10M raised studio-wide).

AI-native by default: Built a multi-agent investor-sourcing engine with a continual-learning loop (ranked by geography, thesis, and stage; enriched via Clay) that produced a pipeline of hundreds of investors, plus a Claude-based operations platform spanning CRM, call transcripts, email, calendar, and contracts that auto-drafts memos, meeting agendas, and proposals.

items.title: Venture Fellow / Investment Diligence
items.org: Atento Capital  —  VC arm of the George Kaiser Family Foundation ($100M+ fund)
bullets: Sourced 14 of 20 finalists and 4 of 7 startups selected to pitch — the most of any of ~12 fellows; screened hundreds of companies.
Ran screening calls, diligenced founding teams, assessed commercial viability, and authored investment theses presented to internal committees.
Recalled by principals to diligence two Core Fund I deals: bottom-up market sizing, competitive analysis, and financial-projection review delivered directly to a GP.
Judgment, validated: On all three deals I diligenced, I recommended against — on scalability, market size, and an unconvincing expansion story; all three are no longer operating.

items.title: Self-Directed Thesis — Deep Technology & American Dynamism
bullets: Developing a four-mega-trend framework for the next decade of value creation — inevitable trends where missing infrastructure creates outsized opportunity: (1) the Autonomous Economy — physical AI, robotics, and automation; (2) Physical America — reindustrialization and onshoring the capacity to build new physical products at speed and scale; (3) Energy Abundance — the power foundation under all of it; (4) Intelligent Infrastructure — the connective layer that makes the rest run. Maps directly onto AI, defense, energy, and space.
Built the view through real work: ~100 founder and operator interviews across energy, manufacturing, and industrials to map emerging trends, bottlenecks, and where capital is missing.

Core conviction: durable returns lie in deep technology — physical AI, automation, energy, and the industrial stack — not in another thin-moat AI application. The U.S. lacks China's ability to spin up new physical products at scale, fast; closing that gap is a generational opportunity.
Fluent in SBIR/STTR non-dilutive funding processes; engaged with OCAST and Oklahoma Catalyst, which administer those programs.

items.title: Founding President — starTUp (University of Tulsa VC Club)
bullets: Founded TU's first VC club and placed members as analysts on the 46VC Hurricane Venture Fund, a multi-million-dollar student/alumni fund.

items.title: Chair, Macroeconomic Committee — TU Student Investment Fund ($8M)
bullets: Implemented an ETF strategy and decision process still in use today; made buy/sell calls on macro trends and led analysts to execute.

items.title: Master of Business Administration — Finance & Entrepreneurship
B.S. Exercise Science / Pre-Med
items.org: The University of Tulsa  —  Division I Cross Country & Track and Field (team finished 5th at NCAA Championships)
